turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#87CEEB" />
  <rect y="420" width="512" height="92" fill="#6BBF4A" />

  <!-- Tower main body -->
  <rect x="156" y="120" width="200" height="300" rx="12" fill="#D7D7D7" stroke="#7A7A7A" stroke-width="6" />

  <!-- Crenellations -->
  <rect x="150" y="88" width="44" height="36" rx="4" fill="#D7D7D7" stroke="#7A7A7A" stroke-width="4" />
  <rect x="198" y="88" width="44" height="36" rx="4" fill="#D7D7D7" stroke="#7A7A7A" stroke-width="4" />
  <rect x="246" y="88" width="44" height="36" rx="4" fill="#D7D7D7" stroke="#7A7A7A" stroke-width="4" />
  <rect x="294" y="88" width="44" height="36" rx="4" fill="#D7D7D7" stroke="#7A7A7A" stroke-width="4" />
  <rect x="342" y="88" width="44" height="36" rx="4" fill="#D7D7D7" stroke="#7A7A7A" stroke-width="4" />

  <!-- Shadow on left side -->
  <path d="M156 120 C176 160, 176 360, 156 420 L186 420 C196 360,196 160,186 120 Z" fill="#000000" opacity="0.08" />

  <!-- Horizontal stone lines -->
  <g stroke="#BFBFBF" stroke-width="4" opacity="0.9">
    <line x1="166" y1="170" x2="346" y2="170" />
    <line x1="166" y1="210" x2="346" y2="210" />
    <line x1="166" y1="250" x2="346" y2="250" />
    <line x1="166" y1="290" x2="346" y2="290" />
    <line x1="166" y1="330" x2="346" y2="330" />
    <line x1="166" y1="370" x2="346" y2="370" />
    <line x1="166" y1="410" x2="346" y2="410" />
  </g>

  <!-- Narrow windows / arrow slits -->
  <rect x="244" y="140" width="24" height="48" rx="6" fill="#2E2E2E" />
  <rect x="244" y="200" width="24" height="48" rx="6" fill="#2E2E2E" />
  <rect x="244" y="260" width="24" height="48" rx="6" fill="#2E2E2E" />
  <rect x="244" y="320" width="24" height="48" rx="6" fill="#2E2E2E" />

  <!-- Door with arch -->
  <path d="M216 420 v-64 a40 40 0 0 1 80 0 v64 z" fill="#6F3F20" stroke="#4B2A17" stroke-width="4" />
  <ellipse cx="256" cy="420" rx="70" ry="12" fill="#BFBFBF" opacity="0.12" />

  <!-- Small details: mortar dots -->
  <g fill="#BFBFBF" opacity="0.8">
    <circle cx="180" cy="190" r="3" />
    <circle cx="200" cy="230" r="3" />
    <circle cx="220" cy="270" r="3" />
    <circle cx="300" cy="150" r="3" />
    <circle cx="320" cy="190" r="3" />
    <circle cx="340" cy="240" r="3" />
  </g>

  <!-- Optional small flag pole to suggest height -->
  <rect x="312" y="64" width="4" height="36" fill="#7A7A7A" />
  <path d="M316 68 q16 10 0 20 z" fill="#C62828" stroke="#8B1E1E" stroke-width="2" />
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Crafting SVG Colors and Structure**

I'm creating a simple SVG design with light gray for the tower and darker gray for the outlines, ensuring no use of scripts or foreignObject. I’ll produce a JSON string with proper escaping, and I think using newline escapes might simplify the process. The SVG will include rectangles for the tower and its base, and I’ll ensure crenellations are present, positioned correctly on top without any comments to keep it clean.**Designing Tower Elements**

I’ve decided to create a top for the tower using a path for battlements, which will include a series of small rectangles in the same fill as the tower. The top platform will be a rectangle that I’ll position accordingly. For the crenellations, I think I could draw small darker rectangles positioned above the top. Then, for the doors, I’ll create an arch shape using a path. I’ll focus on getting the door coordinates right with a semi-circular top for correctness.**Creating Tower Details**

I'm designing the tower with small vertical rectangles for windows, using a fill of "#333." For the shadows, I'll add a darker overlay on the left side with an opacity of 0.1. I’ll also include thin horizontal stone lines at regular intervals. 

The main tower dimensions are x=156, y=120, width=200, height=300, and I'll position the crenellations with five evenly spaced small rectangles on top, starting at y=92 to achieve the battlement effect. I'll place horizontal stone lines at y=170, 210, 250, and so on.**Finalizing Tower Dimensions**

The tower's height of 300 ends at 420, with the final line positioned at 410 near the base. For the door, I’ll create a path that will give an arch with a top at y=356 and a radius of 40. 

For the windows, I’ll probably position three small rounded rectangles down the center, at y=150, 210, and 270. I think I’ll add a small flag pole on top, though I won’t include text. Finally, I’ll construct the SVG string, ensuring proper JSON formatting and escaping.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }
